headline: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Delivered a 39,000x Return Since 1965. He Still Tells Most Investors to Buy Index Funds Instead.
summary: Although Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK-B) has compiled the single best long-run track record in modern markets, the man who built it spends most of his shareholder letters telling you not to try this at home. headline: Bank Of America Links Crypto ETF Exposure With Gen Z Money Habits
summary: Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) has disclosed significant holdings in cryptocurrency-related ETFs and recent adjustments to those positions. The bank has also released new Better Money Habits research on Gen Z financial behavior, including savings patterns and financial independence. These updates highlight how a major US bank is approaching digital assets and younger customers' money habits at the same time. headline: Dollar Gains, Tied to Iran War, May Test Stock Rally Over the Coming Weeks
summary: The U.S. dollar has been quietly making a sustained advance on global currency markets amid a surge in global energy prices and a stunning comeback rally in the stock market.  The dollar’s gains, in fact, could extend over the coming weeks, and start to weigh on domestic stock performance over the back half of the year, with the Iran war effectively closing the Strait of Hormuz and sending oil prices higher.  The U.S. dollar index, which tracks the greenback against a basket of six major global currency peers, was marked 0.06% higher on Wednesday and trading at 99.03, nearly at levels seen in early April.

content.title: Dollar Gains, Tied to Iran War, May Test Stock Rally Over the Coming Weeks
content.summary: The U.S. dollar has been quietly making a sustained advance on global currency markets amid a surge in global energy prices and a stunning comeback rally in the stock market.  The dollar’s gains, in fact, could extend over the coming weeks, and start to weigh on domestic stock performance over the back half of the year, with the Iran war effectively closing the Strait of Hormuz and sending oil prices higher.  The U.S. dollar index, which tracks the greenback against a basket of six major global currency peers, was marked 0.06% higher on Wednesday and trading at 99.03, nearly at levels seen in early April.
